canonical_question: How do you implement the circuit breaker pattern for ERP API integrations?
aliases: Circuit breaker for ERP API calls
How to prevent cascading failures in ERP integrations
ERP API circuit breaker configuration — thresholds, states, fallbacks
When should I use circuit breaker vs retry vs bulkhead for ERP APIs?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Polly v8 removed legacy CircuitBreakerPolicy, replaced with ResiliencePipeline (2023)

constraints: Circuit breaker does NOT replace retry — combine both with retry inside breaker
One breaker per ERP endpoint not per ERP system — wrong granularity defeats the purpose
State is per-process unless externalized to Redis — horizontal scaling requires shared state
Open circuit must have fallback strategy (queue, cache, error)
ERP maintenance windows will repeatedly trip breakers — schedule suppression windows
Half-open probes must be idempotent to avoid duplicate records

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs retry with exponential backoff only, no cascading failure concern
User needs distributed transaction coordination across ERPs
User needs rate limiting or throttling, not failure detection
User needs dead letter queue patterns for failed messages

related_to.label: Dead Letter Queue Patterns for ERP Integration

solves.label: Saga Pattern — circuit breaker protects individual saga steps

alternative_to.label: Batch vs Real-time — batch eliminates some circuit breaker needs

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
What language or platform are you using?
What failure mode are you protecting against?
